info.title: mem9 Server API
info.description: OpenAPI specification for the mem9 Go server routes registered by server/internal/handler/handler.go.

tags.name: Webhooks v1alpha2
tags.description: API-key and Space Chain webhook management endpoints.

post.description: Creates a new tenant. The endpoint has no request body. Optional UTM query parameters are captured when present.

get.description: Validates X-API-Key against the control-plane state and returns whether the key is active or inactive.

post.summary: Create or ingest memory data for a tenant

201.description: Pinned memory created from explicit content.

get.summary: Get a tenant memory by ID

post.summary: Create an async tenant import task

get.summary: List raw session messages for one or more sessions

get.description: Returns runtime-state facts for agent plugins. This route uses local API-key status resolution and does not depend on tenant data DB resolution. Hosted deployments read provider state through the configured runtime usage provider. Provider-disabled self-host deployments return a local fallback response with the same public shape.

201.description: Pinned memory created from explicit content.

get.summary: List raw session messages for one or more sessions

201.description: Created webhook endpoint and one-time signing secret.

200.description: Webhook endpoint and one-time signing secret.

201.description: Created webhook endpoint and one-time signing secret.

post.summary: Rotate secret for Space Chain webhook

200.description: Webhook endpoint and one-time signing secret.

ApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
ApiKeyAuth.description: API key used by v1alpha2 routes.

TenantID.name: tenantID
TenantID.description: Tenant identifier in v1alpha1 tenant-path routes.

XMnemoAgentId.name: X-Mnemo-Agent-Id
XMnemoAgentId.description: Optional per-agent identity header used as the default agent_id when a request body or form does not provide one.

IfMatch.name: If-Match
IfMatch.description: Optional expected memory version for optimistic update.

MemoryQuery.name: q
MemoryQuery.description: Natural-language search query. When omitted, the endpoint lists memories by filters.

MemorySearchMode.name: search_mode
MemorySearchMode.description: Set to `keyword` to search memory content by direct substring match for list filtering. Omit for the default recall-style query behavior.

Limit.name: limit
Limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return. Values <= 0 or > 200 are replaced by the server default.

Offset.name: offset
Offset.description: Pagination offset. Negative values are treated as 0.

SortBy.name: sort_by
SortBy.description: Sort field used when listing memories. All-types lists support updated_at; content, memory_type, and tags require an explicit memory_type.

SortDir.name: sort_dir
SortDir.description: Sort direction used when listing memories.

ScanAll.name: scanAll
ScanAll.description: When using a Space Chain API key, search every Space Chain node instead of stopping after a high-confidence result. Ignored for ordinary Space keys.

SessionIDRepeated.name: session_id
SessionIDRepeated.description: One or more session IDs. Repeat the query parameter for multiple sessions. Maximum 100 IDs.

LimitPerSession.name: limit_per_session
LimitPerSession.description: Maximum messages returned per session. Must be positive and cannot exceed 500.

AppID.name: appId
AppID.description: Optional application isolation filter. Omit appId to search across all appIds. Use appId=<value> for an exact appId, and appId=null or appId= to search only the default/global appId stored as an empty string.

ETag.description: Memory version returned as a decimal integer string.

CreateMemory.description: Create an explicit memory from content or ingest messages. Provide either content or messages, not both.

value.content: The project uses TiDB as its primary database.

messages.content: Remember that deploys use make docker.

CreateImport.description: Multipart upload for async file ingest. File size is limited to 50 MiB. The upload may return 202 before background processing; for file_type=memory, any memories[].metadata.external_provenance member is reserved and causes the import task to fail without writing that file.

RuntimeState.description: Plugin-visible runtime quota state. Provider-disabled deployments return the same shape with notMetered budgets.

BadRequest.description: Invalid request. For v1alpha2 API-key routes this also includes missing, invalid, or inactive X-API-Key values returned by the authentication middleware.

Retry-After.description: Seconds after which the client can retry.

ServiceUnavailable.description: Write conflict or temporarily unavailable backend.

RuntimeAccessBlocked.description: Runtime access is blocked by the configured runtime usage provider. The body follows ErrorResponse, includes a stable runtime quota error classification under details.errorCategory, and exposes public runtime quota fields under details.runtimeQuota when provider details include public quota fields.

PostQuotaRateLimited.description: Runtime usage provider returned a temporary runtime quota rate limit, such as post-quota throttling.

Retry-After.description: Seconds or HTTP date after which the client can retry, forwarded from the runtime usage provider when supplied.

MemoryListBudgetExceeded.description: The explicit all-types memory scan exceeded its server-owned page, row, or elapsed-time budget.

X-Request-Id.description: Canonical request ID for correlating the budget error with server logs.

MemoryRouteRateLimited.description: Memory route rate limit. The response can be a generic API rate-limit envelope from global middleware or a runtime quota rate-limit envelope from the runtime usage provider.

Retry-After.description: Seconds or HTTP date after which the client can retry, when supplied by the generic API rate limiter or runtime usage provider.

providerId.description: Optional provider discriminator. Clients can use this value to decide whether to interpret provider-specific providerData fields.

providerData.description: Optional upstream provider details. Returned independently of providerId; clients should interpret provider-specific fields only when they recognize providerId.

quantity.description: Unit quantity, such as request, USD, or a provider-defined value.

scale.description: Raw values are expressed in this scale. For example, request uses scale 1 and USD cents use scale 100.

percent.description: Used percentage for limited budgets when the provider can compute it.

message.description: Optional runtime-state usage notice for hosted mem9 clients.

CreateMemoryRequest.description: Provide either content or messages, not both. memory_type is only accepted for content requests. mode is only accepted for messages requests. The reserved metadata.external_provenance envelope is optional, creation-only, and accepted only for a messages request containing exactly one fact-eligible user message; invalid reserved envelopes are rejected before asynchronous acceptance.

content.description: Explicit memory content. Required when messages is absent.

agent_id.description: Agent identity. Defaults from X-Mnemo-Agent-Id when omitted.

appId.description: Optional application isolation id. Omitted, null, empty, or whitespace values are stored as the default/global appId; non-empty values are trimmed and stored exactly.

messages.description: Conversation messages for ingest. Required when content is absent.

sync.description: When true, wait for ingest completion up to the server timeout. When false or omitted, ingest runs asynchronously.

disableSessionSave.description: When true on message ingest requests, skip raw session message persistence and only run fact extraction and reconciliation. Defaults to false.

file.description: File to import. Maximum size is 50 MiB.

agent_id.description: Agent identity. Defaults from X-Mnemo-Agent-Id when omitted.

session_id.description: Optional session ID associated with session imports.

CreateImportRequest.description: Multipart upload for async file ingest. File size is limited to 50 MiB. For JSON memory/session import files, include top-level appId or per-memory appId fields to assign imported rows to an application isolation id. The HTTP upload may return 202 before background validation; a memory import containing memories[].metadata.external_provenance fails as a task because the reserved envelope is accepted only through validated message ingest.

message.description: Optional runtime-state usage notice for hosted mem9 clients.

MemoryCreateResponse.description: Created memory with optional runtime-state usage notice fields.

message.description: Optional runtime-state usage notice for hosted mem9 clients.

appId.description: Application isolation id. Empty string represents the default/global appId.

score.description: Search relevance score when returned by search endpoints.

confidence.description: Confidence score when returned by recall/search.

relative_age.description: Human-readable recency string populated for query-time search results.

appId.description: Application isolation id. Empty string represents the default/global appId.

Metadata.description: Raw JSON metadata. The server accepts any valid JSON value. Generic update metadata cannot create, replace, or remove the creation-only external_provenance field; metadata updates to a provenance-bearing memory must therefore be objects. New fact responses may contain the exact agent9/message-source@1 envelope, while historical values are returned as stored and consumers must treat unrecognized provenance shapes as opaque.

CreateMemoryMetadata.description: Raw JSON metadata for memory creation. Object metadata may carry the optional reserved external_provenance envelope; all other object members remain application-defined.

ExternalProvenance.description: Reserved creation provenance. This exact envelope is accepted only on message ingest with exactly one fact-eligible user message and is copied to every newly created memory revision.

type.description: Generic client behavior for the action.

providerActionCode.description: Provider-defined action code. The official/reference runtime usage provider currently emits claimApiKey, upgradePlan, enableOnDemand, increaseSpendingLimit, and resolveAccountState; clients should treat any other value as an opaque provider hint.

severity.description: Provider-defined action severity. Clients should treat unknown values as opaque hints.

url.description: Provider URL for type=openUrl. Present only when supplied by the provider response.

mode.description: Provider-defined quota mode. Clients should treat unknown values as opaque hints.

reason.description: Provider-defined quota reason. Clients should treat unknown values as opaque hints.

RuntimeQuotaError.description: Runtime quota error response. This follows the shared ErrorResponse shape, includes a stable runtime quota error classification under details.errorCategory, and exposes public runtime quota fields under details.runtimeQuota when provider details include public quota fields.

errorCategory.description: Stable runtime quota error category for clients that need one routing condition.

RuntimeMeter.description: Runtime usage meter id supplied by the provider. Clients should treat unknown values as opaque labels.

RuntimeQuotaErrorDetails.description: Public runtime quota details assembled from provider reservation details. Specific provider quota reasons are carried by quotaGateResult.reason.

windowDurationSeconds.description: Provider-defined rate-limit window duration in seconds.

scope.description: Provider-defined rate-limit scope. Clients should treat unknown values as opaque labels.
